title: Student Records
description: Student records in Conroe ISD are protected under clear guidelines about who can access them, how they are maintained, and how long they are kept. Parents and eligible students have the right to review educational records, request corrections, and control who outside the school can see their personal information. Understanding these rules helps families feel confident that their children's academic and personal information is handled with care and respect for their privacy.

timeline_local.summary: Policy FL (Student Records) was updated to add a new item #16 under Types of Education Records, specifically adding 'Records relating to school library materials the student obtains from a school library.' The existing item 16 (Other records) was renumbered to 17.
changes_added: 16. Records relating to school library materials the student obtains from a school library.

timeline_local.notes: Item #16 appears underlined in the document, indicating it is newly added text. The previous item 16 ('Other records that may contribute to an understanding of the student') has been renumbered to 17. This is page 1 of 3 of the FL Local Policy, labeled as Policy Update Page Number 17, for Conroe ISD. The action is inferred as adoption based on the triage hint and meeting date of 2025-10-21.

description_local: This policy governs how the school district collects, maintains, and manages student records, including what types of documents count as official education records. It applies to all students enrolled in the district, as well as their parents or guardians who have rights related to accessing and protecting those records. A recent update now specifically includes library records as an official type of education record, which matters to families because it clarifies that information about a student's library activity is formally protected under the district's student privacy rules.
